data.title: Corteva unveils leadership team for planned seed and genetics spin-off
data.description:
Corteva Inc. (CTVA) on Tuesday named the executive leadership team that will run its planned seed and genetics spin-off, currently referred to as “SpinCo,” ahead of a targeted separation in the fourth quarter of 2026.

Chief Executive Chuck Magro is set to lead the new publicly traded company as chief executive following the split.

The announcement outlines a senior team that includes David Johnson as chief financial officer, Judd O’Connor as chief commercial and operations officer, and Sam Eathington as chief technology officer, along with Audrey Grimm as chief people officer, Brian Lutz as chief digital and information officer and Jennifer Johnson as chief legal officer.

The spin-off is expected to focus on seed and crop genetics, including areas such as gene editing and molecular breeding, while maintaining Corteva’s (CTVA) existing germplasm base.

The company plans to expand commercialization and licensing efforts and explore applications beyond traditional row crops.

The leadership team will formally assume their roles upon completion of the separation, and executives are scheduled to host an investor day at the New York Stock Exchange on September 15, with additional details, including the new company’s name and branding, to be announced.

data.title: Wheels Up announces 1-for-20 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Wheels Up Experience (UP) said on Tuesday that the company will effect a 1-for-20 reverse stock split of its Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share.
  • The company is effecting a stock split to regain compliance with the NYSE's listing standards and qualify for inclusion in the Russell 3000.
  • Wheels Up's common stock is expected to begin trading on the NYSE on a reverse split-adjusted basis on April 27, 2026.
  • UP -16.97% premarket to $0.45.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Goldflare announces shares issued in settlement of debt
data.description:
• Goldflare Exploration (GOFL:CA)  announced on Tuesday that the board of directors has approved the issuance of 547,410 common shares at a deemed price of $0.06 per share to settle $32,844.52 in outstanding accounts payable.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Evolv extends, expands security partnership with Crypto.com Arena
data.description:
• Evolv Technologies (EVLV) on Tuesday said that it had renewed and expanded a multi-year agreement with Crypto.com Arena to deploy its security screening systems.

  • The renewal extends the arena’s 2024 use of Evolv Express concealed weapons detection systems and adds Evolv eXpedite, an autonomous bag-screening system, at a designated entrance.

  • Evolv said the expanded deployment is aimed at improving entry efficiency and screening operations at the Los Angeles venue, which hosts sports and live entertainment events.

  • EVLV +0.94% premarket to $6.4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Iran war will costs taxpayers $1T - Harvard professor
data.description:
The war in Iran will cost U.S. taxpayers at least $1T, according to Linda Bilmes, a public finance expert at Harvard Kennedy School.

“I am certain we will reach one trillion dollars for the Iran war,” the professor said in an interview published April 7. “Perhaps we have already racked up that amount.”

The staggering figure encompasses short-term combat expenses, as well as medium-term reconstruction costs and long-term obligations including veterans’ care that will extend decades into the future. Consumers are increasingly feeling the Iran war when they pull up to the gas station, with gasoline prices recently hitting multi-year highs.

According to Bilmes, the Pentagon initially reported the first three to five days of the conflict cost $11.3B, but her calculations put the true figure at $16B. She estimates the U.S. is spending roughly $2 B daily in upfront costs alone. The Iran war started at the end of February.

The professor noted the Pentagon significantly underestimates expenses by reporting costs based on historical inventory values rather than current replacement prices. Tomahawk missiles valued at $2M in inventory cost $3.0M - $3.5M to replace, while Patriot missiles have jumped from $1.0M - $2.0M to $4.0M - $5M for newer versions.

Medium-term costs include repairing more than 20 damaged U.S. military installations and signing multi-year contracts with defense suppliers to expand production capacity.

The long-term burden will be heaviest of all. With roughly 55K troops deployed and exposed to toxins and environmental hazards, Bilmes warned that veterans’ disability benefits alone could total tens or hundreds of billions of dollars.

“Before this war started, we already owed $7.3 trillion dollars just in disability benefits alone to veterans of previous wars who are alive now,” she said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: SAP, Asana, monday.com downgraded by Piper Sandler
data.description:
SAP (SAP), Asana (ASAN), and monday.com (MNDY) were downgraded by Piper Sandler on Tuesday for various reasons.

SAP was lowered to Neutral from Overweight and the price target was cut to €170 from €220, as analysts at the firm said there have been select discussions that have suggested “slower cloud conversions [near-term] than expected.” Additionally, Germany-based SAP has “outsized exposure” to a tougher European economy, and there is a concern about execution risk as SAP transitions to consumption.

“Our channel conversations suggest steady demand, but we heard elevated anecdotes of slower cloud conversions ahead of the 2027 deadline, largely a result of customer prioritization of general AI adoption over ERP migrations and migrations to date being lower-hanging fruit (higher % of on-prem base dragging feet with conversions than expected). This reduces our conviction in SAP’s ability to deliver upside to the initial FY26 guide,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients.

Asana and monday.com were lowered to Neutral from Overweight, and their respective price targets were cut to $7 and $85 from $9 and $100, as collaboration software companies are in “the penalty box,” the analysts said.

“The collab space has seen greater scrutiny given concerns around seat expansion, cited PLG headwinds, and generally slowing growth rates,” Piper analysts wrote in the note. “ASAN has heightened exposure to the tech vertical, where we could see faster frontier model-driven efficiencies and seat constraints.”

And while monday.com guided to growth of 18.3% in fiscal 2026, it is also seeing margin compression. Conversely, Asana may be in “a no man’s land of growth/profitability (sub-100% NRR, not GAAP profitable yet),” the analysts explained. 

“Increased software scrutiny from investors could temper [near-term] multiple expansion,” the analysts added.

data.title: Citigroup Q1 earnings beat on strong growth in markets, wealth units
data.description:
Citigroup (C) stock rose 1.5% in Tuesday premarket trading after the bank reaffirmed its 2026 net interest income guidance and posted Q1 earnings and revenue beats, driven by robust performance in its Markets and Wealth businesses.

The lender still expects full-year 2026 NII, ex-markets, to rise ~5%-6% from 2025’s $49.8B and an efficiency ratio of ~60% for the year. Furthermore, "we remain very much on track to deliver the 10-11% RoTCE target this year," Chair and CEO Jane Fraser said.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.06, beating the average analyst estimate of $2.63, climbed from $1.19 in Q4 2025 and $1.96 in Q1 2025.

Q1 revenue of $24.6B, topping the $23.6B consensus, increased from $19.9B in the prior quarter and $21.7B a year ago.

Net interest income of $15.7B, exceeding the consensus of $14.0B, was flat Q/Q and increased 12% Y/Y.

Q1 provision for credit losses increased to $2.81B from $2.22B in the prior quarter and $2.72B a year ago.

Total operating expenses of $14.3B vs. $13.8B in the previous quarter and $21.6B in the year-ago period.

End-of-period loans rose to $762B from $752B in Q4. EOP deposits of $1.45T grew from $1.40T at the end of Q4.

"We’ve entered into the final phase of our divestitures, and 90% of our transformation programs are now at or near our target state," Citi's (C) Fraser said. "We demonstrated our commitment to returning capital by repurchasing $6.3 billion shares during the quarter."

Revenue by segment:

  • Services: $6.10B, down 3% Q/Q and up 17% Y/Y.

  • Markets: $7.25B, surging 57% Q/Q and rising 19% Y/Y.

  • Total Banking, which includes Investment Banking and Corporate Lending: $1.72B, down 5% Q/Q and up 13% Y/Y. Investment Banking revenue of $1.33B slipped 2% from Q4 and increased 19% Y/Y, reflecting growth in Advisory and Equity Capital Markets, partially offset by a decline in Debt Capital Markets.

  • Wealth: $3.06B, up 7% Q/Q and 11% Y/Y, driven by growth in Citigold and Retail Banking and the Private Bank, partially offset by lower revenues in Wealth at Work.

  • U.S. Consumer Cards: $4.76B, up 4% Q/Q and Y/Y, driven by growth in net interest income and non-interest revenue.

The beat came as no surprise to Seeking Alpha analyst Steve Booyens of Pearl Gray Equity and Research. "Market services have delivered telling results amid a repositioning in global capital, hedging, and higher cross-asset volatility," he said. "Moreover, NII conditions have been favorable, leading to robust margins. Although credit losses remain contained, Citi has set aside more reserves, suggesting contingency planning amid growing global macro risks. Provisions might settle higher if macro risks persist and lending rates increase."

He added, "That being said, much of the impact will likely be offset by higher NII margins, under the assumption that liquidity conditions remain intact. Citi's capital adequacy is significant, even after buffers are considered. Capital adequacy might be influenced if Citi executes its plan to acquire a range of regional banks due to a goodwill impact. However, I don't think capital adequacy is a risk worth considering at this stage."

Conference call at 11:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: Stock index futures advance on hopes of U.S.-Iran peace talks
data.description:
Stock index futures gained on Tuesday as investors grew optimistic about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks that could ease tensions in the Middle East.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) rose +0.60%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) advanced +0.38%, and Dow futures (INDU) edged up +0.20%.

The gains followed comments from President Donald Trump indicating that Iran had reached out for potential negotiations, even as the U.S. moved forward with a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Asian equities also rallied on hopes that a deal could lower oil prices and support global growth.

The March U.S. Producer Price Index came in at +0.5% M/M vs. +1.2% consensus and +0.5% prior (revised from +0.7%), +4.0% Y/Y vs. +4.7% consensus and +3.4% prior.

Core PPI (excludes foods and energy): +0.1% M/M vs. +0.5% consensus and +0.3% prior (revised from +0.5%), +3.8% Y/Y vs. +4.2% consensus and +3.8% prior (revised from +3.9%).

U.S. Treasury yields edged lower across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slipped to 4.28%, down 1.3 basis points. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell to 3.77%, also down 1.3 basis points, while the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) dipped to 4.88%, declining 1.6 basis points.

data.title: ClearBridge Emerging Markets Strategy adds GFI and MNTHF; exits Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics
data.description:
• The ClearBridge Emerging Markets Strategy (MCEIX) underperformed its MSCI Emerging Markets benchmark in Q1 2026.
  • During Q1 2026, the fund initiated new positions in Gold Fields (NYSE: GFI), Minth Group (MNTHF), and Samsung Heavy Industries.
  • In Q1 2026, the fund exited Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics.
  • Source.

data.title: Lockheed Martin expands venture fund to $1B to back defense tech
data.description:
Lockheed Martin (LMT) on Tuesday said it will expand its corporate venture capital arm, Lockheed Martin Ventures, to $1 billion, more than doubling the fund’s previous $400 million capacity in what the company described as its largest increase since the unit was established in 2007.

The additional capital is expected to be deployed over time to support the development of technologies tied to national security, with a focus on advancing early-stage innovations toward operational use within the defense industrial base.

Chief Financial Officer Evan Scott said the venture arm plays a role in identifying and developing emerging technologies that can be integrated into the company’s broader portfolio. He said the investments are intended to help build a pipeline of technologies relevant to future defense needs.

Lockheed Martin Ventures has invested more than $500 million across over 120 companies since its inception, according to the company. More than 60 of those firms have gone on to become suppliers, collectively receiving more than $750 million in contracts from Lockheed Martin.

The fund has targeted areas such as quantum computing, artificial intelligence, autonomy, directed energy, advanced materials and microelectronics. Over the past two years, the company added 25 firms to its portfolio.

The venture unit was launched with $100 million in initial funding and has operated continuously for nearly two decades, making it one of the longer-running corporate venture funds in the aerospace and defense sector.

Lockheed Martin (LMT) said it plans to continue using the fund to identify technologies that could transition into its supply chain and broader defense programs. The company is also preparing to host its annual Demo Day in August at its headquarters, where portfolio companies and startups will present their work to company executives and technical teams.
